relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jesus moves through cities and villages announcing the Divine reign.
Women healed and released travel with him, including Mary Magdalene.
Joanna, Susanna, and many others support the mission from their resources.
Crowds gather and Jesus teaches through parable.
The sower casts seed along the way.
Some seed withers on rock without moisture.
Some grows among choking thorns.
Good earth bears fruit a hundredfold, and ears are called to hear.
The disciples ask about the parable.
The mysteries of the Divine reign are given through hearing that truly hears.
The seed is the word of the Divine.
The path-heart loses the word before trust can root.
Rocky hearers receive joy but lack root under testing.
Thorns are worries, wealth, and pleasures choking completion.
Good and noble hearts hold the word and bear fruit with endurance.
A lamp is lit to reveal light.
Hidden things move toward visibility.
How one hears shapes what can be received.
Family arrives but cannot reach him through the crowd.
His mother and brothers stand outside seeking him.
True kinship hears and does the word.
Jesus calls the disciples across the lake.
He sleeps as storm and danger rise.
He wakes, rebukes wind and water, and calm arrives.
The storm asks where trust is.
They arrive in Gerasene territory.
A man living among tombs meets Jesus in deep fragmentation.
The hidden forces recognize the Son of the Most High.
Human chains failed where release was needed.
The name Legion reveals manyfold fragmentation.
The troubling forces plead against the abyss.
The herd on the mountain becomes the exit point.
The forces leave the man and rush into the lake.
The keepers report what happened.
The man sits clothed, sound-minded, and at Jesus’ feet.
Witnesses report how he was saved/healed.
Fear asks Jesus to leave.
The restored man wants to remain with him.
Jesus sends him home as witness to divine mercy.
The returning Jesus is welcomed by a waiting crowd.
Jairus falls at Jesus’ feet for his house and daughter.
An only daughter and pressing crowd surround the path.
A woman has spent her life resources seeking healing.
She touches the fringe and is immediately healed.
Jesus seeks the person inside the crowd-touch.
Power has gone out from him.
The woman comes trembling and tells the truth publicly.
He calls her daughter and sends her into peace.
Death-news arrives before the healing path is complete.
Fear is answered by trust and promised saving/healing.
Only the inner witnesses and parents enter.
Jesus speaks sleep where mourners see finality.
They laugh because they know death.
He takes her hand and calls, Child, rise.
Her spirit returns, she rises, and food confirms life.
Astonished parents are told to guard the mystery.
